title: Torshi Liteh (Persian Mixed Pickle)
description: A tangy, deeply flavored Persian condiment made from finely minced eggplants, carrots, and herbs, fermented in vinegar and fragrant spices.

instructions.text: Dice the peeled eggplants. Place them in a pot with 1 cup of the apple cider vinegar and a pinch of salt. Simmer over low heat until soft and cooked through. Drain any excess liquid and let cool.
In a food processor, finely mince the cooked eggplants, carrots, celery, and garlic cloves. The texture should be very fine but not a complete puree.
Transfer the vegetable mixture to a large bowl. Stir in the finely chopped fresh mint and fresh parsley.
Add the turmeric, black pepper, and salt. Mix thoroughly.
Gradually stir in the remaining apple cider vinegar until the mixture reaches a thick, spoonable consistency.
Transfer the mixture to sterilized glass jars. Press down lightly to remove air bubbles. Seal tightly.
Store in a cool, dark place for at least 1 week to allow the flavors to mature before consuming. Move to the fridge after opening.

tips: The finer the mince, the better the Torshi Liteh. Always use a clean, dry spoon when taking some out to prevent mold growth.
storage_notes: Unopened jars can last in a dark, cool place for months. Once opened, refrigerate and consume within 2 months.
serve_with: Rich meat dishes like Lubia Polo, Kebabs, or hearty stews.

substitutions.note: Any sharp vinegar works. Grape vinegar is traditional in Iran.
You can use dried versions, but reduce the quantity by half.
